name: Old Fashioned Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add the brown sugar, molasses, and condensed milk to the melted butter. Stir until well combined.
3. Increase the heat to high and bring the mixture to a boil, stirring constantly.
4. Remove from heat and carefully add the chopped chocolate, stirring continuously until fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
5. Return the saucepan to low heat and continue stirring until the caramel forms a soft ball when dropped in cold water (about 235°F/112°C on an instant-read thermometer).
6. Stir in the vanilla extract and chopped nuts.
7. Pour the caramel into a greased baking dish and spread it out evenly.
8. Allow the caramel to cool slightly, then let it firm up completely before cutting into squares.
Source: Pennsylvania Dutch Cook Book - Fine Old Recipes, Culinary Arts Press, 1936.
